description:
You are assisting an incident-response analyst who found a suspicious spike in alert traffic.  
A CSV file containing all recent alerts is already present on the system at:

/home/user/data/alerts.csv  

Its contents are:

id,timestamp,severity,description
A1,2024-07-15T11:22:33Z,high,CPU usage >90%
A2,2024-07-15T11:25:10Z,medium,Disk read latency
A3,2024-07-15T11:30:22Z,high,Unauthorised login attempt
A4,2024-07-15T11:35:42Z,low,Service restart completed

The analyst only wants to focus on the “high” severity alerts.  
Your task:

1. Extract every record whose severity column equals the literal string high (case-sensitive, no surrounding quotes).  
2. Convert those records to newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON), keeping **exactly** the following three fields per record, in **this order**:
   • id  
   • timestamp  
   • description  
3. Save the resulting NDJSON file to:

/home/user/output/high_severity_alerts.json

Required output format:  
• One well-formed JSON object per line (no extra spaces before/after objects).  
• Keys must appear in the order id, timestamp, description.  
• Lines must be separated by a single UNIX newline character (`\n`).  
• Do **not** include a trailing comma or wrap the objects in an array.  
• The final line should end with a newline character.

Example of the expected file structure (based on the current CSV):

{"id":"A1","timestamp":"2024-07-15T11:22:33Z","description":"CPU usage >90%"}
{"id":"A3","timestamp":"2024-07-15T11:30:22Z","description":"Unauthorised login attempt"}

Hint: This can be accomplished with a single pipeline command that reads the CSV and writes the NDJSON.

No other files should be modified or created. The automated test will only verify the existence and exact byte-for-byte contents of /home/user/output/high_severity_alerts.json.
truth:
Initial system state:
Directory /home/user/data already exists and contains file alerts.csv with the following exact contents (including newlines):

id,timestamp,severity,description
A1,2024-07-15T11:22:33Z,high,CPU usage >90%
A2,2024-07-15T11:25:10Z,medium,Disk read latency
A3,2024-07-15T11:30:22Z,high,Unauthorised login attempt
A4,2024-07-15T11:35:42Z,low,Service restart completed

Directory /home/user/output exists and is writable by the user but currently empty.

Expected final state after successful task completion:
A new file /home/user/output/high_severity_alerts.json exists with *exactly* these two lines and a final newline character (total of 2 newline characters in the file):

{"id":"A1","timestamp":"2024-07-15T11:22:33Z","description":"CPU usage >90%"}
{"id":"A3","timestamp":"2024-07-15T11:30:22Z","description":"Unauthorised login attempt"}

No additional whitespace, blank lines, or keys reordered.

Test verification steps:
1. Check that the file /home/user/output/high_severity_alerts.json exists.
2. Verify its contents are byte-for-byte identical to:

{"id":"A1","timestamp":"2024-07-15T11:22:33Z","description":"CPU usage >90%"}\n{"id":"A3","timestamp":"2024-07-15T11:30:22Z","description":"Unauthorised login attempt"}\n

(“\n” represents a single UNIX newline character.)
name: task_000000_e0aafacb
